listitem.para: The &command; to run to adjust routing and/or firewalling when the status of the connection changes. By default, &command; is passed to /bin/sh -c (see &sh.1;) and run synchronously. See updown-config= for how this can be changed.

&command; may include positional parameters separated by white space (this requires enclosing the whole string in quotes). When invoked using &sh.1;, &command; may include metacharacters (although is is considered unwise).
An example to enable routing when using the XFRM stack, one can use:
To disable calling an updown script, set it to the empty string, eg leftupdown=.
Connections with type= set to passthrough, reject or drop never run ipsec _updown.
For more information, see the command &ipsec-updown.8; and the option updown-config=.
Relevant only locally, other end need not agree on it.
